title: Is there a way to use podman load, for images saved by docker?
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

It is unclear to me based on the documentation if podman can load docker images that are saved by the docker cli. 
I have searched and checked: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md

I have locally run a test and it seems the answer is no? 


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo docker save --output ubuntu.tar ubuntu`
2. 

```
sudo podman load < ubuntu.tar

Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)
```

If this is expected, what does the documentation mean when it says:

> podman load loads an image from either an oci-archive **or a docker-archive stored on the local machine into container storage** 

Also, would a `podman pull` be the **only way** to get a docker image loaded into podman? 






**Describe the results you received:**
Podman does not load the tar archive generated from docker. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect podman to load the tar archive generated from docker. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
sudo podman --version
podman version 3.2.0-dev
```
**Output of `docker version`:**

```
 docker --version
Docker version 20.10.3, build 48d30b5
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS

comments.body:
Closing this issue because this appeared to be happening because my:

```
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.conf
```

were not correctly configured. 

After fixing those files, loading docker images from podman worked.

Might be worth making a more meaningful error if possible though? 

> Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats (oci, oci-archive, dir, docker-archive)

Was not clear that I had an issue in:

```
/etc/containers/policy.json
/etc/containers/registries.conf
```
@KCSesh, please could you give an indication as to what wasn't correct in those files. I'm having the same issue.
